title: Cybersecurity in the Age of Instant Software
content:
AI is rapidly transforming software development, leading to the concept of "instant software" – applications created and deleted on demand. This shift has profound implications for cybersecurity, as AI systems become increasingly adept at both discovering and patching software vulnerabilities.

The arms race between attackers and defenders will be significantly shaped by AI. While attackers may leverage AI for automated exploitation of vulnerabilities, defenders can utilize similar AI capabilities for rapid flaw detection and patch creation. The future could see AI-integrated development processes that minimize vulnerabilities in new software, moving towards a "self-healing" network.

However, challenges remain with legacy software and the patching process. The lag between patch release and adoption, especially in large organizations, presents an ongoing window for attackers. Furthermore, the potential for AI systems themselves to be compromised through methods like prompt injection introduces new complexities. The ultimate impact of AI on cybersecurity will depend on the continued evolution of these technologies and the collaborative efforts between defenders.
